title: Frame-accurate video player via mediabunny (hybrid)
description: Root cause (research-confirmed, 4 angles): <video>.currentTime is NOT frame-accurate by spec — Chromium drives it from the audio clock not video PTS, plus float->int PTS truncation gives ~0.5% drift (the 24-vs-23.98 mismatch behind the stuck-playhead / no-movement-on-step / loop-freeze / out-cant-reach-end bugs). No amount of currentTime math fixes it. FIX: add mediabunny (WebCodecs demux+decode, 1.33M dl/wk, MPL-2.0, Remotion-sponsored). HYBRID: native <video> for PLAY; mediabunny VideoSampleSink -> exact decoded frame on a canvas overlay for PAUSED/frame-step/trim-playhead. DELETE all seek-math: frameStep currentTime+0.25*fs bias, _effectiveFps guessing, _handleRangeBoundary seek dance. Caveat: mediabunny has no built-in .seek yet (#303) — trivial for our short clips (tiny GOP, decode-from-keyframe near-instant). Must frame.close() every VideoFrame (GPU mem). Spans MpiVideoSurface, MpiVideoControlBar, MpiTrimBar + new frame-sink layer.
